person.name: Alice de Tosny
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Alice de Tosny

«b»Parentage«/b»
Alice de Toeni, daughter of Roger (born about 1235), of Flamstead, etc, and Alice, daughter of Humphrey de Bohun, Knt., Earl of Hereford and Essex. They married "by agreement dated 30 June 1239"; their son and heir was born in 1255. Alice's birth date is unknown: her father is said to have died in 1264, so it will have been before 1265. He married again after her mother's death but there is no firm date for this marriage and it could conceivably have been shortly before he died.

«b»Marriage«/b»
Alice de Tony married Walter de Beauchamp, Knt. in or about 1269 (date of settlement). Alice's maritagium included the manor of East Coulston, Wiltshire, which had previously been part of the maritagium of her mother, Alice de Bohun. Walter was born before 1242 (adult in 1263).

On 9 Kalends October, 2 Nicholas IV [23 September, 1289], at Rieti, Pope Nicholas IV commissioned the bishop of Worcester to grant a dispensation to Walter de Bello Campo [Beauchamp] and Alice, daughter of Ralph de Touny, of his diocese, to remain in the marriage they contracted in ignorance that they were related in the fourth degree of kindred, declaring their offspring legitimate.

«b»Issue«/b»
«b»Alice and Walter had seven sons and four daughters:«/b»
1.) Walter de Beauchamp, Knt. Sheriff of Warwicksire and Leicestershire, 1316-1318. Married Hawise, no issue.

2.) John de Beauchamp. Younger son. Before 1318 his brother Walter granted him the manors of Powick and Bransford for his life and that of his mother.

3.) William de Beauchamp, Knt. Keeper of Clarendon Forest, 1308, Sheriff of Worcestershire, 1316-18. Married Joan, one son, William, living in 1334. Fought in Flanders 1297 and in Scotland 1298 and 1306. 1328 Heir to his older brother Walter.

4.) Humphrey de Beauchamp, Clerk.

5.) Ralph de Beauchamp.

6.) Giles de Beauchamp. Received manors from his older brother Walter, 21 May 1329 married Katherine de Bures, daughter and heiress of John de Bures, Knt.

7.) Roger de Beauchamp.

8.) Pernel de Beauchamp.

9.) Eleanor de Beauchamp married (1) John le Boteler, of Wem, Shropshire, (2) John de la Mare, Lord del la Mare.

10.) Margaret de Beauchamp, married Robert de Lisle, Knt, 1st Lord Lisle of Rougemont.

11.) Maud de Beauchamp, elected Abbess of Godstow Abbey, Oxfordshire, in 1335. She was living in June 1346, but died in or before 1349, when her successor was elected.

«b»Death of Walter«/b»
Sir Walter de Beauchamp died testate at Chalfont, Buckinghamshire on 16 February 1302/3 and was buried in the Grey Friars at Smithfield on 24 February 1302/3. 

«b»Widowhood«/b»
In 1309 Walter's widow, Alice, was granted protection for one year, she going on a pilgrimage beyond seas.

In 1310 she was summoned to answer Edward Burnell of a plea wherefore she made waste and sale of the lands, houses, woods, and gardens which she had in the wardship of the inheritance of the said Edward in Broom Court near Bidford, Warwickshire to his disherison.

In 1313 Alice was disputing the advowson of Kemerton, Gloucestershire with Hawise, widow of John de Ferrers.

Alice presented to the church of Kemerton, Gloucestershire in 1332.

In 1334 she was cited by the Pope to appear as witness regarding the matter of the consanguinity existing between her kinsfolk, John de Bohun, Earl of Hereford and Essex, and his wife, Margaret Basset.

Alice was living in 1347.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Tosny於大約1254年出生時，她的父親Roger de Tosny20歲，母親Alice de Bohun20歲。她於1280年之前與Walter de Beauchamp結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1324年1月1日在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素享年71歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾福拉姆斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tosny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean Pierre.
Americanized form of any of various surnames ultimately derived from the Latin personal name Antonius (see Anthony ), for example Greek Antoniou and Italian Toni .
French: from the personal name Tony, a pet form of Antonin (from Latin Antoninus, a derivative of Antonius; see Anthony ).

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Tosny was born about 1254, her father, Roger de Tosny, was 20 and her mother, Alice de Bohun, was 20. She married Walter de Beauchamp before 1280. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 1 January 1324, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Flamstead, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle est décédée le 1 janvier 1324, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Flamstead, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosnyが約1254年に生まれた時，父Roger de Tosnyは20歳，母Alice de Bohunは20歳であった。1280年以前にWalter de Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1324年1月1日にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで71歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアフラムステアドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Tosny(이)가 대략 1254년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Tosny(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Bohun(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1280년 이전 Walter de Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1324년 1월 1일 영국 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 플램스테아드에 묻혔다.
